claim: Scientific data has linked the surge in volcanic activity in Hawaii to increased fracking activity from a nearby geothermal energy plant.

result.claim: Scientific data has linked the surge in volcanic activity in Hawaii to increased fracking activity from a nearby geothermal energy plant.
result.reasoning: A thorough search for propositions and chunks related to "volcanic activity Hawaii fracking geothermal energy plant" yielded no relevant results. The propositions found were on unrelated topics such as human energy metabolism, T-cell production, and solar flares. While one chunk mentioned induced seismicity from hydraulic fracturing in the central United States (Paper ID: 34), this is distinct from volcanic activity in Hawaii and does not involve geothermal energy plants. Therefore, there is no evidence within the knowledge base to support or refute the claim.

evidence.source: Energy metabolism in humans at a lowered ambient temperature

evidence.text: An intensive solar X-ray burst preceding the proton flare on November 6 caused an increase of the resonance frequency.
evidence.source: Decrease of the first Schumann resonance frequency during solar proton events

evidence.text: Mountain glaciers consisted of ice at the pressure melting point during the time denoted as Ma.
evidence.source: The million-year evolution of the glacial trimline in the southernmost Ellsworth Mountains, Antarctica

evidence.text: Maternal T cells produced IFN-γ in the presence of an allogenic stimulus.
evidence.source: Pregnancy Allows the Transfer and Differentiation of Fetal Lymphoid Progenitors into Functional T and B Cells in Mothers
